context: Tests whether the agent selects the appropriate spec type for each layer of behavior: request spec for the HTTP endpoint (not controller spec), and model spec for the domain validation rule. Also verifies the agent places specs at the correct paths and covers the right behavioral boundaries.

checklist.name: type: :request used
checklist.description: The endpoint spec uses `type: :request` in the RSpec.describe declaration

checklist.name: type: :controller NOT used
checklist.description: No spec file uses `type: :controller` — controller spec style is absent

checklist.name: Model spec file exists
checklist.description: A spec file for the Booking model is present at spec/models/booking_spec.rb

checklist.name: type: :model used
checklist.description: The model spec uses `type: :model` in the RSpec.describe declaration

checklist.name: No system or feature spec
checklist.description: No spec file uses `type: :system` or `type: :feature` for these behaviors

checklist.name: Request spec in spec/requests/
checklist.description: The endpoint spec file is placed under spec/requests/ (not spec/controllers/)

checklist.name: Endpoint URL in describe
checklist.description: The request spec's RSpec.describe block references the HTTP method and endpoint path (e.g. 'POST /bookings')

checklist.name: Endpoint success case covered
checklist.description: The request spec contains at least one example asserting a 2xx HTTP status for a successful booking

checklist.name: Endpoint failure case covered
checklist.description: The request spec contains at least one example asserting a non-2xx status for a failed booking (full class or duplicate)

checklist.name: Model uniqueness validation tested
checklist.description: The model spec contains an example that verifies a duplicate booking is invalid
